channel.title: Sagittarius Eye Podcast: Elite Dangerous' leading news network
channel.itunes:subtitle: Created by Commanders, for Commanders
channel.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
channel.itunes:summary: Sagittarius Eye is Elite Dangerous' leading news network — created by Commanders, for Commanders.
channel.description: With a history of publishing excellence, a top-notch team of talented contributors, galactic reach, and peerless coverage and insight, Sagittarius Eye is your indispensable companion to the Milky Way. We are Sagittarius Eye — we see all…

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 38
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: July 3307
item.itunes:summary:
This month, we take a cruise to Colonia, wonder where the Thargoids came from, check out some ship-launched fighters, lock and load some assault rifles, and get to know the galaxy's favourite marine mammals.

00:00:22 - Editorial. Read by Rosetta Stone

00:02:26 - Flying the fleet. Written by Mack Winston, read by Rheeney, Poet Sparrow and Spidey002

00:27:40 - Small arms: assault rifles. Written by Osashes, read by Qaztar

00:37:28 - Ship launched fighters. Written by Ariri, read by Professor Getter

00:46:16 - Interview: The Hull Seals. Read by Catisfaxion and Moogyver

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 37
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: June 3307
item.itunes:summary:
In this issue, we recap the tumultuous history of the Hesperus, discover what influences the colours of atmospheres, revisit spacefaring history with the Apollo 15 Expedition, speculate about the Soontill relics, and pull the pin on some hand grenades.

00:00:18 - Editorial. Read by Qatzar

00:02:28 - Small Arms: Grenades. Written by Osashes, read by Catisfaxion

item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: May 3307
item.itunes:summary:
In this issue, we take the Orca for a spin, check in on the search for Raxxla, perfect our cold-orbiting, get in the flight seat of the majestic Imperial Cutter, and get to know Arf's favourite ships.

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 35
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: April 3307
item.itunes:summary:
In this issue, we discover the origins of the Thargoid war and the Anti-Xeno Initiative, recap the tumultuous trial of Admiral Vincent, take a trip to Bering Port, get to know the remarkable Erimus Kamzel, get to grips with the new range of personal plasma weapons, and find out what Commander Gambit loves to fly.

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 34
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: March 3307
item.itunes:summary:
In this edition, we take a look at why the Empire is having so much trouble with the NMLA despite its firepower and galactic influence. Then, to cap off our series of articles on SRV racing, we spill the secrets of the trade, and show you how to drive an SRV like you’ve never driven one before. Not content with the capabilities of the SRV, we have a guide on how best to make use of the glowing balls of death that spew forth should you install a plasma accelerator. Finishing off this issue, we have an ode to flight assist.

00:00:19 Editorial. Read by Wrangler Actual

00:02:10 Trouble with Marlinists. Written by Mack Winston, read by Beetlejude

00:15:02 An Advanced SRV Driving Lesson. Written by Osashes, read by Spidey002

00:25:35 A hard look at hard points: Plasma accelerators. Written by Ariri, read by Moogyver

00:32:41 Poetry: In Praise of Flight Assist. Written by Adurnis, read by Poet Sparrow

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 33
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: February 3307
item.itunes:summary:
In this edition, our intrepid reporter sets out to win the Scarab Mastery Championship, a competition that brings out the best in Vodel’s humble surface recon vehicle. We also get to fly the Imperial Courier, a truly marvellous machine that will elegantly both leave your friends and enemies in your wake as you hurtle through space. Then we go on a trip to Aquila's Halo with the Eagles and Angels expedition on a voyage of scientific discovery.

00:00:18 Editorial, read by Beetlejude

00:02:26 The Scarab Mastery Championship. Written by Osashes, read by Wrangler Actual with Eid LeWeise and Blackmaze

00:17:27 The Imperial Courier: A Homing Missile Capable of Frame Shift. Written by Ariri, read by Catisfaxion

00:24:41 Of Eagles and Angels. Written by Andrew Gaspurr, read by Scott Cleverdon with Wotherspoon and Kai Zen

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 32
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: January 3307
item.itunes:summary:
In this edition, we take a look at the highly ambitious project to place fleet carriers all over the galaxy, as ports of call for explorers tens of thousands of light years from home. We also visit the unremarkable star system, LFT 509 - unremarkable, that is, except for its unexplained permit lock, and the possibility that it might hold the secrets of the Dark Wheel. Or at least, some forgotten pirate treasure. Also, in our continuing “Hard Look at Hardpoints” series, we give a hard look at one of the most fearsome weapons in the combateer’s arsenal: the railgun.

00:00:18 Editorial. Read by Scott Cleverdon.

00:02:40 A galaxy-spanning network: the Deep Space Support Array. Written by Adurnis, read by Poet Sparrow and Spidey002

00:22:05 The Mystery of LFT 509. Written by Mack Winston, read by Wotherspoon

00:34:51 A hard look at hardpoints: Railguns. Written by Ariri, read by Moogyver

The Mystery of LFT 509. Written by Mack Winston, read by Wotherspoon

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 31
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: December 3306
item.itunes:summary:
In this edition, our writer participates in the Quarantine Cup Races, which pits the Viper against the Eagle to discover which ship can prove itself to not only be the fastest in a straight line, but have the agility to make it around a challenging race circuit. Continuing on the theme of small fast ships, we take a look at the Sidewinder, a ship most commanders wanted to leave behind as quickly as they could, but perhaps this ship’s surprising turn of speed might make us want to think again. Turning to deep space, we discover the breadth and depth of the Cygnus constellation, one of the true wonders of our galaxy, and finally, we finish up with the next installment of our Hard Look at Hard Points, and see what the multicannon can do for the aspiring combat pilot.

00:00:18 Editorial. Read by Rheeney

00:02:06 The Quarantine Cup Races. Written by Osashes, read by WranglerActual, Rheeney and Buur

00:11:59 Ships you wanted to forget: The Sidewinder. Written by Mack Winston, read by Rosetta Stone and Kai Zen

00:25:07 The Cygnus Constellation. Written by Andrew Gaspurr, read by Beetlejude

00:40:22 A Hard look at hard points: Multicannons. Written by Ariri, read by Catisfaxion

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 30
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: November 3306
item.itunes:summary:
In this edition, we take a look at a bitter conflict in the Colonia nebula, far away and unknown by most commanders. What drives this conflict, and why is it even occurring? We talk to some of those behind it to find out why, and what scores are being settled. We then move on to the second part of our investigation of the Dark Wheel and its motivations. It’s not all conflict, though, as our intrepid reporter heads out on the Banana Nebula Expedition, categorising the notable stellar phenomena, which are common in the region. We finish off with the Eight WHeel Drive Endurance Championship, a race that requires rare skill to even complete, let alone win.

00:00:19 - Editorial. Read by Kai Zen

00:03:24 - The Carcosa War. Written by Adurnis, read by Catisfaxion, Scott Cleverdon, Poet Sparrow, Eid LeWeise and Spidey002

00:22:39 - Spokes of the Wheel Part 2. Written by M Lehman, read by Beetlejude, Kai Zen, Eid LeWeise and Wotherspoon

00:33:05 - Banana Nebula Expedition. Written by CMDR Raf Von Thorne, read by Wotherspoon and Scott Cleverdon

00:44:39 - The Eight Wheel Drive Endurance Championship. Written by CMDR BlackMaze, read by Moogyver, Scott Cleverdon, Kai Zen and Eid LeWeise

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 29
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: October 3306
item.itunes:summary:
In Issue 29, we take a look at some mysteries, and how you may understand them. We open with the first in a series on the Dark Wheel, as M. Lehman takes a look at the origins of this shadowy organisation. Andrew Gaspurr gives us insight of what’s so special about Lalande 28607. Ariri discusses what lasers you might want to fit to your ship, and finally, Mack Winston takes a good look at the often overlooked Saud Kruger Dolphin, and why you should fly one.

00:29:34 - Small Luxuries in deep space: the Dolphin. Written by Mack Winston, read by PoetSparrow

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 28
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: September 3306
item.itunes:summary:
In Issue 28, we take a good look at how the galactic limits were finally reached by what can only be described as an epic journey taken by Cmdr Jonathan Burnage and his compatriots. M. Lehman follows this up with a visit to the Mother of Redemption, Mack Winston has some practical tips on conflict zones, and finally Cmdr Ariri takes us on a tour of degenerate matter: the stuff that makes up white dwarfs and neutron stars./p>

item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: Q1 3306
item.itunes:summary:
In Issue 26, we meet a group dedicated to flying with flight assist off, learn how to take part in a CG and live to talk about it, revisit the life of one of the Empire's colourful figures, discover an insurgency, recap two ongoing expeditions, ask what makes a brown dwarf, and swot up on some lore.

item.title: Sagittarius Eye - The Elite Dangerous Magazine - Issue 25
item.itunes:author: Sagittarius Eye
item.itunes:subtitle: Q4 3305
item.itunes:summary:
In Issue 25, we get to know three Pilots Federation groups: the East India Company, the Chapterhouse of Inquisition and the Fatherhood. We crunch the numbers on some of the most fearsome weapons in the void, learn about a mad new sport, and ask: just why does society hate the Pilots’ Federation?

In Issue 24, we get up close and personal with life on airless worlds, do some supercruise maths, get to know one of the Federation’s most influential families, head out to the Carina constellation, and steal some art.
